name: Power Supply Component
description: The `power_supply` component allows you to have a high power mode for certain outputs. For example, if you're using an [ATX powersupply](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ATX) to power your LED strips, you usually don't want to have the power supply on all the time while the output is not on. The power supply component can be attached to any [Output Component](https://esphome.io/components/output/) and will automatically switch on if any of the outputs are on. Furthermore, it also has a cooldown time that keeps the power supply on for a while after the last output has been disabled.

config_entries.label: Pin
config_entries.description: The GPIO pin to control the power supply on.

config_entries.label: Mode
config_entries.description: Pin mode flags (input / output / pullup / pulldown / open_drain). Combine flags as needed — e.g. input + pullup for a button pulled to VCC.

config_entries.label: Open Drain
config_entries.description: Set the open drain mode flag.

config_entries.label: Inverted
config_entries.description: Invert the logical level. ``true`` swaps high/low in software so an active-low button reads as active when grounded.

config_entries.label: ID
config_entries.description: The id of the power supply so that it can be used by the outputs.

config_entries.label: Enable On Boot
config_entries.description: If the power supply should be enabled when the power supply component is setup. Defaults to false. The startup delay will be applied (other component setup will be blocked until the delay has elapsed.) This is useful for power supplies that will never be turned off and avoids the need to specifically configure the power supply in a different component.

config_entries.label: Enable Time
config_entries.description: The time that the power supply needs for startup. The output component will wait for this period of time after turning on the PSU and before switching the output on. Defaults to `20ms`. Maximum of less than `5s`.

config_entries.label: Keep On Time
config_entries.description: The time the power supply should be kept enabled after the last output that used it has been switch off. Defaults to `10s`.
